title: PMBOK 8 Principles, Domains & Mindset
summary: How the PMBOK Guide 8th Edition is built - 6 principles (the why) and 7 function-oriented performance domains (the what, each with processes and ITTOs) - plus the 7th-to-8th changes and how to answer from it.

sections.heading: How PMBOK 8 is built
sections.body: PMBOK 8 stays principle- and outcome-based but is function-oriented: 6 principles set behaviour, and 7 performance domains each embed concrete processes with ITTOs. The headline change is that sustainability becomes a core principle.

blocks.title: How to use this
lines: Principles = the why: behavioural guardrails for judgement.
Performance domains = the what: function areas with processes and ITTOs.
Answer from the principles and value, then tailor to context.
Exam domains (People / Process / Business Environment) are the ECO's, separate from these PMBOK domains.

items.text: Adopt a holistic, systems view - components and their interdependencies. Merges the 7th-Ed System Interactions and Navigate Complexity.
Value is the ultimate measure of success. Absorbs stakeholder engagement, adaptability, resiliency and change enablement.
Embed quality into processes AND deliverables. Refined from 'Build Quality'; adds sustainability and compliance.
Be an accountable leader - integrity, self-awareness, shared leadership. Merges stewardship and leadership.
Integrate sustainability across all project areas - environmental, social and economic (triple bottom line). Entirely NEW; the biggest 8th-Ed addition.
Build an empowered culture across the whole stakeholder community. Evolved from 'Collaborative Project Team'.

blocks.title: Exam tip
lines: When two options both look valid, pick the one that delivers value and honours a principle.
Sustainability is now a first-class lens, not a footnote.

sections.heading: The 7 performance domains
sections.body: Function-oriented - each domain embeds specific processes with ITTOs (34 processes in total).

rows.value:
9 processes. Initiating, Planning, Executing, M&C, Closing.
Integration, execution coordination, change control, knowledge, QA, sourcing, close-out.

6 processes. Planning, M&C.
Requirements, WBS, validation.

4 processes. Planning, M&C.
Earned value lives here (Monitor and Control Finances).

7 processes. Initiating, Planning, Executing, M&C.
Now includes Communications (formerly a knowledge area).

5 processes. Planning, Executing, M&C.
Team and physical resources; 'Lead the Team'.

6 processes. Planning, Executing, M&C.
Renamed from 'Uncertainty'.

blocks.title: Exam tip
lines: Governance is the largest domain (9 processes) and spans all five process phases.
Communications now sits inside Stakeholders; earned value now sits inside Finance.

sections.heading: 7th to 8th Edition mapping
sections.body: If you learned the 7th Edition's 12 principles and 8 performance domains, here is how they map across.

rows.value: Stakeholders. Now includes Communications processes (7 total).
Resources. Team dev/mgmt consolidated into 'Lead the Team'; culture moved to the Empowered Culture principle.
Moved to The Standard (Project Life Cycles). No longer a performance domain.
Distributed. Planning processes now live inside each domain.
Governance. Integration, execution, change control, knowledge, QA, sourcing, close-out.
Scope. Requirements, WBS, validation (QA split to Governance).
Distributed into M&C processes; earned value -> Finance.
Risk. Ambiguity, complexity and volatility now sit under 'Adopt a Holistic View'.

blocks.title: Big picture
blocks.text: The 8th Edition swings back toward concrete process-and-ITTO guidance while keeping the performance-domain framing. The philosophical headline: sustainability becomes a core principle.

sections.heading: How to answer from PMBOK 8
sections.body: The mindset is the principles applied: proactive, value-focused, principle-honouring judgement, tailored to context.

items.title: Read for approach and domain
lines: Predictive, agile or hybrid? Which function domain is in play?

items.title: Prefer the value-delivering action
lines: Proactive, collaborative, within your authority, honouring a principle.

items.title: Diagnose before acting
lines: Follow the process; take the least-drastic correct action.

items.title: Escalate last
lines: Only when the situation is genuinely beyond your authority.

blocks.title: Exam tip
lines: Focus on Value and Be an Accountable Leader break most ties.
Build quality and sustainability in - never bolt them on.

source: PMBOK Guide 8th Edition - 6 principles and 7 performance domains (function-oriented, with processes and ITTOs).
